opening: Dreams are the only place where memory edits itself in real time.

1655.title: Tourist bus surnames
1655.excerpt: On a tourist bus I tell Beijing grandma my last name is Li—I am a lawyer in training.
1655.interpretation: Identity is performed for family in transit. A parallel strand invents hostility toward a public figure and a disappearing parent before a flight—kinship, naming, and disappearance share one night.

1720.title: Cats in cages
1720.excerpt: I ask whether the cats have finally gotten used to cages—the ones you need for the vet.
1720.interpretation: Care appears as adaptation to constraint. A second strand treats music as an art form measured by whether youth will accept the listener.

1742.title: Two named dogs
1742.excerpt: Two dogs: [dog] small and skinny, [dog] larger—named, sized, kept.
1742.interpretation: Companionship is itemized by body and name. The dream barely elaborates; naming is enough.

1762.title: Watermelon neighbor
1762.excerpt: A verge-old friend next door—ball, watermelon, stars—leaves and never comes back.
1762.interpretation: A parallel-universe friendship ends in disappearance and grudge. Reunion at home is mediated by a mother’s joke about who liked whom.

1765.title: Grandpa well again
1765.excerpt: Grandpa is well again in cosplay with grandma—red suit, brown suit—I hug them both.
1765.interpretation: Illness reverses into costume and comedy. A second strand seats the dreamer in a campus comedy club among parents and students.

1770.title: BL mashup
1770.excerpt: BL plot lines mash into tropes; the main figure keeps shifting roles.
1770.interpretation: Story logic becomes a collage of romance tropes—the dream watches genre assemble itself.

1772.title: Editor's song
1772.excerpt: In a very serious situation you hear a song—and all the subtext is already inside it.
1772.interpretation: Metadata appears as audible content: song information must live inside the music itself, collapsing documentation into performance.

1774.title: Roommate packing
1774.excerpt: Dorm door left open—I wake to my roommate packing luggage; she has no exams, I do.
1774.interpretation: Asymmetry of obligation: one person leaves; the other remains inside the testing calendar.

2200.title: California mountains
2200.excerpt: Mountains so colorful I photograph cows and dogs—then learn I must ward off spirits in the mirror.
2200.interpretation: Pastoral abundance flips into night duty. The mirror speaks with a voice that is not the dreamer’s; care for others’ nightmares becomes a private ordeal.

2249.title: Fox and bingo rock
2249.excerpt: A class fox, a numbered golden-green rock like bingo—then a teacher throws the rock away.
2249.interpretation: Rules become accusation. Rain and a grandfather across the street restore recognition after public shame.

2252.title: Animals close up
2252.excerpt: Penguins break ice; rhinos hold peace signs—I tell mom looking at animals makes me happy.
2252.interpretation: Joy is immediate and photographic. A parent’s prayer for grandparents’ recovery sits beside the dreamer’s private knowledge that they are not really getting better.

2263.title: Twin daughters
2263.excerpt: Twin girls with little bobs—mid-dream the logic of kinship collapses, then they are simply happy.
2263.interpretation: The dream stages an impossible family structure, interrupts itself with ethical alarm, then returns to the children’s smiles as if the contradiction could be lived with.

2269.title: Lotus incense plaza
2269.excerpt: Artificial lotuses release incense; torso-shaped bags with past-year designs outshine this season’s crocodile shine.
2269.interpretation: Shopping becomes a museum of surfaces. Taste is comparative—what was better last year haunts what is for sale now.

2271.title: Parallel graduation
2271.excerpt: Same schools, slightly different photos. A childhood voice arrives pitched higher than memory allows.
2271.interpretation: Graduation recurs as a fork in continuity—images and pitch mark a self that is almost the same, almost happier, still accountable to a parent’s complaint.

2273.title: Library aquarium
2273.excerpt: A library that is also an aquarium, with a food court attached—and a worker unkind to everyone.
2273.interpretation: Hybrid civic space stacks reading, display, and consumption into one building. Water and books share a roof; hostility appears at the counter.

2276.title: Holiday drink
2276.excerpt: A new drink I really want—mom says there are already so many at home; I say it’s the holidays.
2276.interpretation: Desire negotiates with household surplus. The holiday licenses wanting one more thing.

2277.title: Funeral practice rooms
2277.excerpt: Office rooms where people practice the post-death process—living loved ones choose what others would eat at the funeral.
2277.interpretation: Death is rehearsed as logistics while the loved one is still alive. Grief appears as menu selection in fluorescent rooms.

2282.title: Mom at the concert
2282.excerpt: I bring mom to a Seventeen concert; idols leave the stage—high-fives, Chinese hello, a tongue-twister line.
2282.interpretation: Idol proximity and parental presence overlap. A performer recalls taking his own mother; the dream mirrors care across the barrier of the stage.

2301.title: Grandpa young again
2301.excerpt: Grandpa talks—he is in his twenties, remembers mom, does not remember me. I cried in the dream.
2301.interpretation: Dementia is rewritten as youth with selective memory. Technology feels new to him; recognition skips a generation and lands as grief.

2306.title: Pitch and gender
2306.excerpt: Raising the key changes a song differently in a male voice than in a female one—words begin to distort.
2306.interpretation: The dream rehearses a research task: compare gendered vocal effects under transposition, then prove it with four examples.

2309.title: Paused park
2309.excerpt: Everything in the amusement park was paused—literally stopped in the air.
2309.interpretation: Process freezes mid-gesture. Talk of stocks and condos sits beside a nearly empty clubhouse where glare makes nearby things invisible.

2311.title: Nine-tailed fox drama
2311.excerpt: A K-drama about religion—a nine-tailed fox gains a tail every fifty years—then breaks the fourth wall.
2311.interpretation: The dreamer is both inside the episode and at a fan viewing. Belief systems accumulate like tails; the show discusses other shows about belief.

2328.title: Worn-out labels
2328.excerpt: Sweatshirts labeled worn out—don’t wear. Rich people never have to donate; they can keep every piece.
2328.interpretation: Clothing becomes an archive of class. Space itself is the privilege that lets nothing be discarded.

2336.title: Concert interface
2336.excerpt: Click the screen and dirt comes off; the interface listens for the concert’s vibrations.
2336.interpretation: A live performance is rewritten as interactive UI. Attention splits between haptic design and a companion who appears only as a confused visual.

2355.title: Temple of rounds
2355.excerpt: At the last second everything reverses—then still explodes. Lights return to the temple seams for another round.
2355.interpretation: Power cycles through sun-and-moon light in maze walls. Time reversal fails to prevent endings; identity resets as lights in the architecture.

2357.title: Traumatic marriage
2357.excerpt: A marriage that the note itself marks as politely traumatic.
2357.interpretation: The dream is almost only a verdict. Partnership appears as a punchline that refuses elaboration.

patterns.label: Hybrid architecture
patterns.summary: Spaces refuse single function: library∩aquarium∩food court; temple∩maze; amusement park that is not Disneyland; funeral offices; shopping plazas as museums of surfaces.

patterns.label: Mediated sound
patterns.summary: Music and voice recur as systems—metadata inside songs, pitch distortion, pitch-shifted childhood speech, concert UIs, idol stages, music judged by youth acceptance.

patterns.label: Suspended or reversed time
patterns.summary: Parks freeze mid-air; temple combat reverses; illness reverses into wellness or youth; memory skips generations.

patterns.label: Parallel identity
patterns.summary: Slightly different graduations, gods who reset as lights, childhood friends in parallel universes, surnames performed for family on a bus.

patterns.label: Performance as interface
patterns.summary: Songs, concerts, dramas, and vocal keys become editable or enterable surfaces—clicked, annotated, high-fived, or broken through a fourth wall.

patterns.label: Kinship and care
patterns.summary: Parents, grandparents, and elders recur as recognition, prayer, complaint, concert companions, or figures who remember the wrong generation.

patterns.label: Animals as witness
patterns.summary: Dogs, foxes, penguins, cows, cats, and fox-spirits mark happiness, classroom games, pastoral wealth, veterinary cages, and mythic accumulation of time.

patterns.label: Class and space
patterns.summary: Wealth appears as closet space, paused condos, mountain houses with cows, and shopping plazas where last year’s design still wins.
